event: 73 years old
female
brought in by her daughter for acute altered mental status
experienced a mechanical fall, and subsequently developed a pruritic scalp
and purulent drainage from sinuses on the scalp behind her right ear
temperature of 97.9°F
heart rate of 127 bpm
respiratory rate of 22 breaths/min
blood pressure of 144/80 mmHg
oxygen saturation of 98% on room air
face, scalp and right ear were erythematous and edematous
large fluctuant mass with purulent drainage over the occipital and parietal portions of the scalp
posterior auricular mass without signs of active drainage
concern for underlying abscesses
hyperglycemia to 700
anion gap metabolic acidosis with serum bicarbonate of 20 and ketonuria
admitted to the intensive care unit for diabetic ketoacidosis and cellulitis of the scalp with concern for underlying abscesses
placed on a continuous infusion of insulin
broad-spectrum coverage with intravenous Vancomycin and Cefepime
underwent incision and drainage of scalp lesion on hospital day 2
revealing a 10-cm subgaleal abscess with large amounts of purulence evacuated
initial admission blood cultures grew MRSA
continued on intravenous Vancomycin and Cefepime
underwent a paranasal sinus CT with intravenous contrast
did not show involvement
underwent a transesophageal echocardiogram
without evidence for endocarditis
returned to the operating room on hospital days 4 and 5
for further pulse irrigation of the wounds and sharp debridement
with the involvement of plastic surgery
continued to have serial bedside debridements
scalp wound ultimately measured 20 cm in length, 10 cm in width and 2 cm depth
right posterior auricular wound measured 7 × 7 × 2 cm
definitively closed with split-thickness skin grafts harvested from the right thigh
100% take of the grafts on postoperative day 7
discharged on hospital day 32 on intravenous Vancomycin
completed a full 7-week course
